target: qr_decomposition (classical Gram-Schmidt QR) used by eval_eig (Eig primitive)

lever.description: The Gram-Schmidt inner loops read Q columns (q[k*n+i]) and the original A column j (a[k*n+j]) with stride n down columns of the row-major matrices (a cache miss per element), and re-extracted A's column j inside the orthogonalization loop for every previous column. Reworked to extract A column j once into a contiguous buffer (col_j) and keep each Q column in a contiguous buffer (q_cols[i]); the dot-product and axpy inner loops are now stride-1. Q is reassembled row-major at the end. The accumulation order over k is unchanged.

benchmarks.method: rch Criterion, eval_primitive_multi(Eig, 48x48 f64); baseline = row-major Gram-Schmidt (HEAD, already with the matmul ikj win), optimized = contiguous-column Gram-Schmidt

after.tool: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'linalg/eig_48x48_f64'

benchmarks.note: Non-overlapping CIs. This lever targets the Gram-Schmidt step that the prior matmul ikj win (mcqr.24) left untouched; cumulative eig speedup vs the original i-j-k+row-major code is ~1.62x (61.5ms -> 37.9ms).

score.note: Realized 1.28x (this lever) on a real production LAPACK primitive (Eig), bit-exact.

isomorphism_proof.floating_point: Dot products accumulate over k in ascending order exactly as before (dot += q_col_i[k]*col_j[k] equals dot += q[k*n+i].0*a[k*n+j]); the axpy v[k] -= dot*q_col_i[k] is unchanged; norm and normalization identical. Bit-for-bit identical q and r.
isomorphism_proof.ordering: Only memory layout of intermediates changed; no reordering of any reduction.
isomorphism_proof.rng: No RNG state is read or written.
isomorphism_proof.errors: Singular-column handling (norm <= 1e-15 leaves the Q column zero) preserved.
isomorphism_proof.bit_identity_proof: New test qr_decomposition_contiguous_bit_identical_to_rowmajor builds a 9x9 matrix and asserts f64::to_bits() equality of q (real+imag) and r vs an inline row-major Gram-Schmidt reference. eig/svd oracle conformance + 1007/1007 fj-lax tests pass. No synthetic golden-bits file: the to_bits parity test pins the result.
